nodes.x: And as Jesus passed by, he saw a man which was blind from his birth.
And his disciples asked him, saying, Master, who did sin, this man, or his parents, that he was born blind?
And when he was come into the house, the blind men came to him: and Jesus saith unto them, Believe ye that I am able to do this? They said unto him, Yea, Lord.
And Jesus stood still, and commanded him to be called. And they call the blind man, saying unto him, Be of good comfort, rise; he calleth thee.
And it was revealed unto him by the Holy Ghost, that he should not see death, before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.
¶ And, behold, two blind men sitting by the way side, when they heard that Jesus passed by, cried out, saying, Have mercy on us, O Lord, thou Son of David.
And his disciples asked him, saying, Master, who did sin, this man, or his parents, that he was born blind?
And as Jesus passed by, he saw a man which was blind from his birth.

vious estate. He was wise in making no reply to the Lord, for doubtless he felt himself unable to do so. But we find that Moses understood the subject, for at the time the children of Israel transgressed, he and his brother Aaron fell upon their faces before the Lord, and Moses, pleading with great power and faith in behalf of the children of Israel, used these words, “O God, the God of the…

It is very evident that this was the belief of the people in the days of the Savior. Even the Apostles and those with Jesus evidently believed in the pre-existence of man. This is manifest from a certain question which they put to Jesus on the occasion of a blind man making his appearance before him. They said to him, “Master, who did sin, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?” In…

he was upon the earth. As he and his disciples passed along the road they found a man who had been blind from his birth. The disciples referred to Jesus and asked, “Who did sin, this man, or his parents, that he was born blind?” If the result of the blindness was the sin of the man, certainly that sin must have been committed before the birth in the flesh. It is scarcely possible that a man would…
Jesus answered, Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents: but that the works of God should be made manifest in him.
¶ Now, lo, if he beget a son, that seeth all his father’s sins which he hath done, and considereth, and doeth not such like,
They answered and said unto him, Thou wast altogether born in sins, and dost thou teach us? And they cast him out.
For he hath made him to be sin for us, who knew no sin; that we might be made the righteousness of God in him.
Our fathers have sinned, and are not; and we have borne their iniquities.
I must work the works of him that sent me, while it is day: the night cometh, when no man can work.
Yea, before the day was I am he; and there is none that can deliver out of my hand: I will work, and who shall let it?
Man goeth forth unto his work and to his labour until the evening.

Then I beheld all the work of God, that a man cannot find out the work that is done under the sun: because though a man labour to seek it out, yet he shall not find it; yea further; though a wise man think to know it, yet shall he not be able to find it.
And whatsoever soul it be that doeth any work in that same day, the same soul will I destroy from among his people.

To-day is to us the most important period of our existence. It rises full of the knowledge of the past, which with warning finger points to the fleeting velocity of the present. It is bigAvith the destinies of the future, which may be to us dark, hopeless, and miserable, or an eternity of felicity and happiness, according as the present is used or abused — abused either by sins of commission or…
As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
And that I am the true light that lighteth every man that cometh into the world;
Behold, I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God. I am the life and the light of the world.

And behold, I am the light and the life of the world; and I have drunk out of that bitter cup which the Father hath given me, and have glorified the Father in taking upon me the sins of the world, in the which I have suffered the will of the Father in all things from the beginning.
I am the light and the life of the world. I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end.
I am come a light into the world, that whosoever believeth on me should not abide in darkness.
When he had thus spoken, he spat on the ground, and made clay of the spittle, and he anointed the eyes of the blind man with the clay,
And he took the blind man by the hand, and led him out of the town; and when he had spit on his eyes, and put his hands upon him, he asked him if he saw ought.
And he took him aside from the multitude, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spit, and touched his tongue;
And some began to spit on him, and to cover his face, and to buffet him, and to say unto him, Prophesy: and the servants did strike him with the palms of their hands.
And when they had blindfolded him, they struck him on the face, and asked him, saying, Prophesy, who is it that smote thee?
They say unto the blind man again, What sayest thou of him, that he hath opened thine eyes? He said, He is a prophet.
And said unto him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam, (which is by interpretation, Sent.) He went his way therefore, and washed, and came seeing.
He answered and said, A man that is called Jesus made clay, and anointed mine eyes, and said unto me, Go to the pool of Siloam, and wash: and I went and washed, and I received sight.

And he answered and spake unto those that stood before him, saying, Take away the filthy garments from him. And unto him he said, Behold, I have caused thine iniquity to pass from thee, and I will clothe thee with change of raiment.
¶ And the word of the Lord came unto him, saying,
And the word of the Lord came unto him, saying,
And he that burneth them shall wash his clothes, and bathe his flesh in water, and afterward he shall come into the camp.
¶ The neighbours therefore, and they which before had seen him that he was blind, said, Is not this he that sat and begged?
¶ And it came to pass, that as he was come nigh unto Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the way side begging:
And some of them said, Could not this man, which opened the eyes of the blind, have caused that even this man should not have died?
¶ They brought to the Pharisees him that aforetime was blind.
Which now of these three, thinkest thou, was neighbour unto him that fell among the thieves?
Some said, This is he: others said, He is like him: but he said, I am he.
Whosoever cometh to me, and heareth my sayings, and doeth them, I will shew you to whom he is like:
Yet ye have not known him; but I know him: and if I should say, I know him not, I shall be a liar like unto you: but I know him, and keep his saying.
Therefore said they unto him, How were thine eyes opened?
Then said they to him again, What did he to thee? how opened he thine eyes?
They say unto him, Lord, that our eyes may be opened.
That thine eyes may be open unto the supplication of thy servant, and unto the supplication of thy people Israel, to hearken unto them in all that they call for unto thee.
Now the eyes of Israel were dim for age, so that he could not see. And he brought them near unto him; and he kissed them, and embraced them.
He answered and said, A man that is called Jesus made clay, and anointed mine eyes, and said unto me, Go to the pool of Siloam, and wash: and I went and washed, and I received sight.
Then again the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight. He said unto them, He put clay upon mine eyes, and I washed, and do see.
And said unto him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam, (which is by interpretation, Sent.) He went his way therefore, and washed, and came seeing.
And Jesus answered and said unto him, What wilt thou that I should do unto thee? The blind man said unto him, Lord, that I might receive my sight.
And the Lord spake unto Enoch, and said unto him: Anoint thine eyes with clay, and wash them, and thou shalt see. And he did so.

he suffered for us,” and so on. What did the Jews or Romans care about all this? Did they believe in him? It appears not, or but very few of them. And, as we have just been hearing, it was the same among the multitudes who followed him; although he fed them, and they saw his miracles, yet they understood nothing of the power by which his mighty works were accomplished. It was just so with the…
Then said they unto him, Where is he? He said, I know not.
And they said unto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare?
And said, Where have ye laid him? They said unto him, Lord, come and see.
Then said they unto him, Where is thy Father? Jesus answered, Ye neither know me, nor my Father: if ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also.
And he said unto him, Yea.
Then said they unto him, Who art thou? And Jesus saith unto them, Even the same that I said unto you from the beginning.
¶ They brought to the Pharisees him that aforetime was blind.
And some of the Pharisees which were with him heard these words, and said unto him, Are we blind also?
And they which were sent were of the Pharisees.
But some of them went their ways to the Pharisees, and told them what things Jesus had done.
And it was the sabbath day when Jesus made the clay, and opened his eyes.
And that day was the preparation, and the sabbath drew on.
And immediately the man was made whole, and took up his bed, and walked: and on the same day was the sabbath.
¶ And now when the even was come, because it was the preparation, that is, the day before the sabbath,
And on the sabbath we went out of the city by a river side, where prayer was wont to be made; and we sat down, and spake unto the women which resorted thither.
But when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto him, Behold, thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do upon the sabbath day.
Then again the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight. He said unto them, He put clay upon mine eyes, and I washed, and do see.
¶ And he turned him unto his disciples, and said privately, Blessed are the eyes which see the things that ye see:
Then came his disciples, and said unto him, Knowest thou that the Pharisees were offended, after they heard this saying?

Therefore said some of the Pharisees, This man is not of God, because he keepeth not the sabbath day. Others said, How can a man that is a sinner do such miracles? And there was a division among them.
And the Pharisees said unto him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful?
¶ Then gathered the chief priests and the Pharisees a council, and said, What do we? for this man doeth many miracles.
And the scribes and Pharisees watched him, whether he would heal on the sabbath day; that they might find an accusation against him.
And he said unto them, The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:
And the Pharisees and scribes murmured, saying, This man receiveth sinners, and eateth with them.
They say unto the blind man again, What sayest thou of him, that he hath opened thine eyes? He said, He is a prophet.
Therefore said they unto him, How were thine eyes opened?
He hath said, which heard the words of God, which saw the vision of the Almighty, falling into a trance, but having his eyes open:
And when he came out, he could not speak unto them: and they perceived that he had seen a vision in the temple: for he beckoned unto them, and remained speechless.
But the Jews did not believe concerning him, that he had been blind, and received his sight, until they called the parents of him that had received his sight.
Because that by reason of him many of the Jews went away, and believed on Jesus.
And in that same hour he cured many of their infirmities and plagues, and of evil spirits; and unto many that were blind he gave sight.
And they asked them, saying, Is this your son, who ye say was born blind? how then doth he now see?
His parents answered them and said, We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind:
Since the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was born blind.
His parents answered them and said, We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind:
And they asked them, saying, Is this your son, who ye say was born blind? how then doth he now see?

"We can make him a father," the Prior answered. An answer which brought
them all to their feet, opposing it loudly:
But by what means he now seeth, we know not; or who hath opened his eyes, we know not: he is of age; ask him: he shall speak for himself.
I say unto you, he hath seen him; nevertheless, he who came unto his own was not comprehended.
He hath said, which heard the words of God, and knew the knowledge of the most High, which saw the vision of the Almighty, falling into a trance, but having his eyes open:
He who hath faith to see shall see.
He hath said in his heart, God hath forgotten: he hideth his face; he will never see it.
These words spake his parents, because they feared the Jews: for the Jews had agreed already, that if any man did confess that he was Christ, he should be put out of the synagogue.
And he went into the synagogue, and spake boldly for the space of three months, disputing and persuading the things concerning the kingdom of God.
Howbeit no man spake openly of him for fear of the Jews.
But the Jews did not believe concerning him, that he had been blind, and received his sight, until they called the parents of him that had received his sight.
They understood not that he spake to them of the Father.
Therefore said his parents, He is of age; ask him.
But by what means he now seeth, we know not; or who hath opened his eyes, we know not: he is of age; ask him: he shall speak for himself.
And he asked his father, How long is it ago since this came unto him? And he said, Of a child.

    even according to this pattern, is not of me;

as young, and wanting tuition, when he came to the crown, since Appian
informs us [Footnote Syriac. p. 177: that he was then but nine years
old.]
Then again called they the man that was blind, and said unto him, Give God the praise: we know that this man is a sinner.
He answered and said, Whether he be a sinner or no, I know not: one thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see.
Jesus said unto them, If ye were blind, ye should have no sin: but now ye say, We see; therefore your sin remaineth.
He answered and said, Whether he be a sinner or no, I know not: one thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see.
Then again called they the man that was blind, and said unto him, Give God the praise: we know that this man is a sinner.
And when they saw it, they all murmured, saying, That he was gone to be guest with a man that is a sinner.
But he that lacketh these things is blind, and cannot see afar off, and hath forgotten that he was purged from his old sins.
Then said they to him again, What did he to thee? how opened he thine eyes?
Then touched he their eyes, saying, According to your faith be it unto you.
And he lifted up his eyes, and saw the women and the children; and said, Who are those with thee? And he said, The children which God hath graciously given thy servant.
And he said unto them, What manner of man was he which came up to meet you, and told you these words?
He answered them, I have told you already, and ye did not hear: wherefore would ye hear it again? will ye also be his disciples?
Then asked he them again, Whom seek ye? And they said, Jesus of Nazareth.
Jesus answered them, Do ye now believe?
And he answered and said unto them, I will also ask you one thing; and answer me:
Jesus answered and said unto them, Go and shew John again those things which ye do hear and see:
And in the house his disciples asked him again of the same matter.
Then they reviled him, and said, Thou art his disciple; but we are Moses’ disciples.
And some of the Pharisees from among the multitude said unto him, Master, rebuke thy disciples.
Then said Jesus to those Jews which believed on him, If ye continue in my word, then are ye my disciples indeed;
His disciples say unto him, Master, the Jews of late sought to stone thee; and goest thou thither again?
Then they suborned men, which said, We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses, and against God.
¶ Then in the audience of all the people he said unto his disciples,
We know that God spake unto Moses: as for this fellow, we know not from whence he is.
¶ Moreover the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
And God spake unto Moses, and said unto him, I am the Lord:
And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,

Behold, I am he of whom Moses spake, saying: A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear in all things whatsoever he shall say unto you. And it shall come to pass that every soul who will not hear that prophet shall be cut off from among the people.
The man answered and said unto them, Why herein is a marvellous thing, that ye know not from whence he is, and yet he hath opened mine eyes.
He answered and said unto them, Because it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given.
And he said, Unto you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of God: but to others in parables; that seeing they might not see, and hearing they might not understand.
Now we know that God heareth not sinners: but if any man be a worshipper of God, and doeth his will, him he heareth.
If we say that we have not sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.
Then he said, I have sinned: yet honour me now, I pray thee, before the elders of my people, and before Israel, and turn again with me, that I may worship the Lord thy God.
Yea, wo be unto him that hearkeneth unto the precepts of men, and denieth the power of God, and the gift of the Holy Ghost!
Since the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was born blind.
For since the beginning of the world have not men heard nor perceived by the ear, neither hath any eye seen, O God, besides thee, how great things thou hast prepared for him that waiteth for thee.
But I, as a deaf man, heard not; and I was as a dumb man that openeth not his mouth.
For since the beginning of the world men have not heard, nor perceived by the ear, neither hath the eye seen, O God, beside thee, what he hath prepared for him that waiteth for him.
If this man were not of God, he could do nothing.
For let not that man think that he shall receive any thing of the Lord.

these and many other like testimonies, I cannot doubt that God has laid no 
impossible command on man; and that, by God's aid and help, nothing is 
impossible, by which is wrought what He commands. In this way may a 
man, if he pleases, be without sin by the assistance of God.
And let him repent of his sins, for he seeketh the praise of the world.
And if you have not faith, hope, and charity, you can do nothing.
They answered and said unto him, Thou wast altogether born in sins, and dost thou teach us? And they cast him out.
Jesus answered them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whosoever committeth sin is the servant of sin.
And when he saw their faith, he said unto him, Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.
Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when he had found him, he said unto him, Dost thou believe on the Son of God?
And I besought thy disciples to cast him out; and they could not.
Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when he had found him, he said unto him, Dost thou believe on the Son of God?
And he brought him to Jerusalem, and set him on a pinnacle of the temple, and said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down from hence:
Christ, His Son, he was cast out of heaven, with all those who partook of his spirit. Were they not all cast out of heaven?
And immediately Jesus stretched forth his hand, and caught him, and said unto him, O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?
When Jesus saw him lie, and knew that he had been now a long time in that case, he saith unto him, Wilt thou be made whole?
He answered and said, Who is he, Lord, that I might believe on him?
And he said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped him.
And he answered: Yea, Lord, I know that thou speakest the truth, for thou art a God of truth, and canst not lie.
And I said unto him: Believest thou the scriptures? And he said, Yea.
And he answered, and said unto him: I do not know what that meaneth.
And he answered and said: Yea, I believe all the words that thou hast taught.
And Jesus said unto him, Thou hast both seen him, and it is he that talketh with thee.
Jesus saith unto him, Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.
And that which he hath seen and heard may be made known unto my disciples, that they perish not. And for this cause have I spoken these things.
And he said unto him, If now I have found grace in thy sight, then shew me a sign that thou talkest with me.
And Jesus said unto him, Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole. And immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus in the way.
And he said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped him.
And the man bowed down his head, and worshipped the Lord.
And he said: Yea, I believe according to thy words.
He answered and said, Who is he, Lord, that I might believe on him?
Then came she and worshipped him, saying, Lord, help me.
And when they saw him, they worshipped him: but some doubted.
¶ And Jesus said, For judgment I am come into this world, that they which see not might see; and that they which see might be made blind.
Wo unto the blind that will not see; for they shall perish also.
And some of the Pharisees which were with him heard these words, and said unto him, Are we blind also?
And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things: and they derided him.
Jesus said unto them, If ye were blind, ye should have no sin: but now ye say, We see; therefore your sin remaineth.
¶ And Jesus said, For judgment I am come into this world, that they which see not might see; and that they which see might be made blind.
If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin: but now they have no cloak for their sin.
